free_text: The d-beam energy was 300 keV and the intensity
was 5 milliamps. a sample transport system of six
pneumatic tubes provided irradiation positions giving
monochromatic neutrons of 13.4-14.9 MeV. Another tube
was put at -105 deg to check the symmetry of the
system. The irradiation positions was about 15 cm
from the tritium target. An additional tube set at
-22.5 deg and at 1.5 cm distance was used when strong
activities were required. The transfer time was about
Neutron energies cover range from 14.9 to 13.7 MeV
deg.  Distance between tritium target and sample is
15 cm. The effective energy of incident neutrons at
irradiation position was determined by the ratio of
Two Ge detectors with efficiencies of 12 and
respectively, were used to measure the induced acti-
vities. Each detector was covered with a 5 mm acrylic
absorber to reduce beta-rays. The efficiency calibra-
tion was performed at a distance of 5 cm using 24Na,
monitoring based on the standard Al-27(n,alpha)
The samples of mass-separated isotopes and samples
of natural abundance were used. Powder samples were
wrapped in powder paper. Sample sizes were (10mm*
70 mg.  Isotopic abundance is given in each Subent
